file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file containing interactive element definitions for the HP Smart home page. Captures all buttons, links, and interactive elements with their locators (CSS selectors, XPath, IDs, data-testid attributes), element properties (text, type, location, size), and visibility states. Used as a reference for test automation to identify and interact with UI elements on the HP Smart landing page including sign-in controls, navigation links, consent buttons, and footer elements.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive UI metadata file for the HP Smart home page containing 32 interactive elements. The file uses a structured JSON format to catalog all UI elements with multiple selector strategies (CSS, XPath, ID, data-testid) for maximum flexibility in test automation. Key authentication elements include multiple sign-in buttons and a create account button. The page includes app download badges for iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS platforms. Navigation elements cover Instant Ink, Printer Setup, Ink purchasing, and Help & Support. The metadata includes precise element positioning and sizing information which can be used for visual regression testing. All elements have auth_priority and form_priority flags to indicate their importance in authentication and form submission flows. The best_selector field provides the recommended locator strategy for each element, typically preferring ID selectors when available for stability.

file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file containing authentication control element definitions for the HP Instant Ink sign-in page. Captures username input field, password input field, sign-in button, use password button, user avatar menu, and sign-out button with their complete locator strategies and element properties. This file serves as the definitive reference for all authentication-related UI interactions in test automation scripts for the Instant Ink application login flow.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This metadata file focuses exclusively on authentication controls for the HP Instant Ink sign-in flow. It implements a two-step authentication pattern where users first enter their username, click 'Use password' button, then enter their password and sign in. The file includes both pre-authentication elements (username, password inputs, sign-in button) and post-authentication elements (user avatar menu, sign-out button). All authentication elements are marked with auth_priority=true indicating their critical role in the login flow. The data-testid attributes on the avatar menu and logout button follow the jarvis-react-consumer-layout naming convention, suggesting this is part of a React-based UI framework. The username and password fields use aria-label attributes for accessibility. This file is essential for automating login, logout, and session management test scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file containing billing and payment form element definitions for HP Instant Ink subscription enrollment. Captures credit card input fields (card number, expiration month, expiration year, CVV), form action buttons (Continue, Cancel), all located within an iframe (pgs-iframe). This file provides complete locator strategies for automating payment information entry during the Instant Ink enrollment process, including iframe context switching requirements.

page_info.page_title: HP Instant Ink – Ink Monthly Subscription | HP Official Site

interactive_elements.inferred_purpose: Credit card number input field for payment information, requires switching to pgs-iframe context
Dropdown select for credit card expiration month, requires switching to pgs-iframe context
Dropdown select for credit card expiration year, requires switching to pgs-iframe context
CVV security code input field for credit card verification, requires switching to pgs-iframe context
Cancel button to abort payment information entry and return to previous step, requires switching to pgs-iframe context
Continue button to submit payment information and proceed to next enrollment step, requires switching to pgs-iframe context

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This metadata file is critical for payment automation as all billing form elements are contained within an iframe (pgs-iframe). Test automation scripts MUST switch to the iframe context before interacting with any of these elements. The iframe information is consistently provided for each element with both CSS and XPath selectors for the iframe itself. The payment form follows standard credit card input patterns with separate fields for card number, expiration month/year dropdown selects, and CVV. The card number field uses autocomplete='cc-number' and CVV uses autocomplete='cc-csc' following HTML5 autocomplete standards. The Continue button has both an ID selector and a data-testid='continue-button' attribute providing multiple locator options. All form fields are marked with form_priority=true indicating their importance in the enrollment flow. The location and size values are all 0, suggesting these elements may be dynamically positioned or the metadata was captured before rendering. This file is essential for automating the billing step of HP Instant Ink subscription enrollment.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive UI metadata file for the HP Instant Ink dashboard page for a specific printer (HP-5CG14833DR). Contains extensive element definitions for dashboard navigation, subscription management controls (cancel, pause, transfer, redeem promo), printer details, plan management, FAQ accordions, account menu items, enrollment flow elements, shipping/billing forms, and cancellation feedback options. This file serves as the complete reference for all dashboard-related UI automation including subscription lifecycle management, account settings, and enrollment workflows.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most comprehensive metadata file in the batch with 78 interactive elements covering the entire HP Instant Ink dashboard experience for a specific printer (HP-5CG14833DR). The file includes complete subscription lifecycle management controls: cancel subscription, pause subscription, transfer to another printer, cancel paper add-on, and redeem promo codes. Navigation elements follow the jarvis-react-consumer-layout pattern with nested menu structures for HP Instant Ink submenu items (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking). The cancellation flow includes both retention ('Keep Instant Ink') and confirmation ('Confirm Cancellation') buttons with data-linkid tracking. Shipping address form fields use data-testid attributes consistently (street1, city, zip-code, state, phoneNumberSmall). The file includes FAQ accordion elements and account menu items (Subscriptions, Orders, Statements, Shipping & Billing, Profile, Users, Notifications). Multiple Continue buttons are defined with various data-testid and data-analyticsid attributes for different enrollment contexts. The printer-specific filename suggests this metadata is tied to a particular printer serial number, indicating the dashboard may have printer-specific variations. All form fields are marked with form_priority=true. This file is essential for comprehensive dashboard automation including subscription management, account settings, enrollment flows, and cancellation workflows.

file_analyses.file_purpose: UI metadata file for the generic HP Instant Ink dashboard page (not printer-specific). Contains similar element definitions as Dashboard-HP-5CG14833DR.json but represents the general dashboard structure. Includes navigation elements, subscription management controls, printer details, FAQ sections, account menu, enrollment flow elements, and shipping/billing forms. Serves as the baseline dashboard metadata for test automation when printer-specific variations are not required.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the generic dashboard metadata file with 76 interactive elements, very similar in structure to Dashboard-HP-5CG14833DR.json but without printer-specific naming. The file covers the same functional areas: navigation, subscription management, enrollment flows, and account settings. Key differences from the printer-specific version include slightly fewer elements (76 vs 78) and the absence of printer serial number in the filename. This file includes plan type selection buttons ('Select Monthly', 'Select Pay As You Print') that may not be present in the printer-specific version if the printer is already enrolled. The enrollment flow elements include HP Checkout button, Add Shipping/Billing buttons, Save button, state dropdown with California option, enrollment radio button, terms of service checkbox, and final Enroll button. The cancellation button uses the same comprehensive set of locators as the printer-specific version. This file serves as the baseline dashboard metadata and should be used for general dashboard automation scenarios where printer-specific variations are not required. The high degree of overlap between this file and Dashboard-HP-5CG14833DR.json suggests the dashboard UI is largely consistent across different printer contexts with only minor variations.
